title: Cirrus: Simplify use of cache in automation
body:
***Depends on:*** #13376 #14180

With the increasingly complex `.cirrus.yml` task relationships, build
cache wasn't always working as intended.  Recently, non-build tasks were
observed assuming authority over `gopath_cache`.  Ref.:
https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13998#issuecomment-1108834538

Address this by an overall simplification using artifacts instead of
cache.  Using artifacts allows establishing concrete
authorship/authority over cached repo. content.  In this way, dependent tasks
may simply consume the artifact with `curl` instead of needing to use
complex algorithms.

Also, add support for new automation library version w/ `$DEBUG` fix.
ref: https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/128

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: BTW I'm not ignoring your review request, or actually I am, but only until CI passes and the churn stops. If you'd like me to look at it while it's still in turmoil, please lmk. Otherwise I'll wait for green CI.

> BTW I'm not ignoring your review request, or actually I am

Oh sorry, I made it a DRAFT PR after I opened it.  Yes, please ignore for now, I'll remove DRAFT when it's ready.

Okay, I think this is ready (tests running as I write this) at least for review.  There may be some test failures & tweaking needed due to switching some tasks from VMs -> containers.  But overall this PR is doing what I intended, eeking out several more minutes of runtime savings and cleaning up the horrible/confusing caching mess in `.cirrus.yml`.

rootless tests is having trouble wit ssh.
Otherwise LGTM, but I'm not a SME by any means.

> rootless tests is having trouble wit ssh

Ya, not sure why that suddenly started happening.  I'm testing out a fix for it.
Force-pushing, rebased and added simplified rootless ssh setup.

[Argh! rootless unit is still borkt.](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5468821833121792).  Thing is, "it works fine for me in a VM" (C).

Sigh...okay, I guess some more tinkering is needed here.

Oh! I found the problem, it's caused by trying to run the rootless unit test inside a container, where of course there is no sshd running and `localhost` means something different.  Okay, force-pushing with that fix and a split of the `$DEBUG` fix into a dedicated commit (in case "use containers" commit needs to ever be reverted).

The rootless unit problem is fixed, and I'm seeing other problems (queuing) due to swiching tasks to use containers.  This is unfortunate, since (absent task-queuing) the container-based tasks run a bit faster than VMs.  I'm going to remove that commit (98ae246f3f0ed99396eeb748f8b7b373b1b501c3) and leave it up to a future effort.
...aaaaand of course there's [another problem](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6312256957841408).  Okay, fixed that and force-pushed again.
Force-push: Rebased + added comments re: ssh setup.
force push: rebased +[ fixed 'artifacts' task failure (log)](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6283796977811456).
Force-push: Rebased + split `Makefile` fix into dedicated PR.
